snp5.8:1.1: There are in the world sages, they say
snp5.8:1.4: Do they call one a sage for possessing knowledge
snp5.8:1.5:
or possessing a way of life?
The Buddha:
snp5.8:2.1: Not on account of his views, learning, or knowledge
snp5.8:2.2:
do the skilled here, Nanda, call one a sage.
Those who live
snp5.8:2.4:
Those, I say, are called sages.
Nanda:
snp5.8:3.3: describe purity in terms of views & learning,
snp5.8:3.4: describe purity in terms of habits & practices,
snp5.8:3.5: describe purity in terms of manifold ways:
snp5.8:3.6: Have they, dear sir, living there in that way,
snp5.8:3.8:
I ask you, Blessed One.
Please tell me.
The Buddha:
snp5.8:4.3: describe purity in terms of views & learning,
snp5.8:4.4: describe purity in terms of habits & practices,
snp5.8:4.5: describe purity in terms of manifold ways:
snp5.8:4.6: None of them, living there in that way,
snp5.8:4.7:
I tell you, have crossed over birth & aging.
Nanda:
snp5.8:5.3: describe purity in terms of views & learning,
snp5.8:5.4: describe purity in terms of habits & practices,
snp5.8:5.5: describe purity in terms of manifold ways:
snp5.8:5.6: If, sage, as you say, they’ve not crossed over the flood,
snp5.8:5.7: then who in the world of beings divine & human, dear sir,
snp5.8:5.9:
I ask you, Blessed One.
Please tell me.
The Buddha:
snp5.8:6.1: I don’t say that all brahmans & contemplatives
snp5.8:6.4: Those here who’ve abandoned what’s seen, heard, & sensed,
snp5.8:6.6: who’ve abandoned their manifold ways —again, all—
snp5.8:6.8:
They are the ones, I tell you, who’ve crossed over the flood.
Nanda:
snp5.8:7.1: I relish, Gotama, the Great Seer’s words
snp5.8:7.3: Those here who’ve abandoned what’s seen, heard, & sensed,
snp5.8:7.5: who’ve abandoned their manifold ways —again, all—
snp5.8:7.7: I, too, say they’ve crossed over the flood.
